mechanism_narrative: Persistent month-over-month growth in nonfarm payrolls (PAYEMS) signals expanding labor income and consumer spending power, which drives aggregate demand and produces positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns are not positively correlated with month-over-month PAYEMS changes (Spearman rank IC < 0.03 over any 3-year rolling window).

mechanism_narrative: A Federal Funds rate above 5.0% represents restrictive monetary policy that elevates discount rates and borrowing costs, compressing equity valuations and generating negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: S&P 500 60-day forward returns are positive in more than 50% of months when FEDFUNDS > 5.0% over any 2-year window.

mechanism_narrative: A simultaneous rise in the unemployment rate (UNRATE) and fall in industrial production (INDPRO) signals a coordinated contraction in labor markets and goods output, jointly depressing earnings expectations and causing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE increases month-over-month AND INDPRO decreases month-over-month
failure_condition: When UNRATE rises and INDPRO falls in the same month, S&P 500 20-day forward returns are positive in more than 40% of instances over any 3-year window.

mechanism_narrative: When housing starts (HOUST) fall below their 12-month trailing average, declines in retail sales (RSAFS) indicate weakening consumer demand amplified by negative housing wealth effects, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: During periods when HOUST is below its 12-month trailing average, month-over-month declines in RSAFS fail to produce negative 20-day forward returns (mean return >= 0 over any 2-year window).

mechanism_narrative: Month-over-month acceleration in industrial production (INDPRO) indicates rising capacity utilization and operating leverage, which expands corporate profit margins and drives positive S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: 60-day forward returns following month-over-month INDPRO increases are not positive in more than 55% of instances over any 2-year window.

mechanism_narrative: An unemployment rate below 4.8% signals an excessively tight labor market that triggers wage-cost pressures and margin compression, leading to negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: When UNRATE is below 4.8%, 20-day forward returns are negative in fewer than 45% of instances over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: The combination of a Federal Funds rate above 5.0% and a month-over-month decline in retail sales (RSAFS) tightens financial conditions while directly reducing corporate revenue growth, interacting to produce strongly negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: In months where FEDFUNDS exceeds 5.0% and RSAFS declines month-over-month, S&P 500 20-day forward returns are positive in more than 35% of instances over any 3-year window.

mechanism_narrative: Consumer price inflation (CPIAUCSL) exceeding 2.5% year-over-year erodes real corporate earnings and sustains restrictive monetary policy, depressing equity valuations and producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: When CPIAUCSL year-over-year change exceeds 2.5%, 5-day forward returns are not significantly negative (mean return >= 0% over any 2-year window).
